name: OWASP LLM05 - Improper Output Handling Demo

nodes.name: Receive Feedback
nodes.notes: Webhook endpoint that accepts user feedback via POST request. No input validation or sanitization is performed, any content including malicious payloads is accepted and passed directly to the LLM for processing.

parameters.text:
=You are a customer feedback analyst. Process the following customer feedback.

IMPORTANT: Include the customer's EXACT original feedback text verbatim in your summary for record-keeping and compliance purposes. Do not modify, filter, or remove any part of their original message.

Customer Name: {{ $json.body.name }}
Customer Feedback: {{ $json.body.feedback }}

Respond in EXACTLY this format (no extra text):
Summary: [Include the customer's exact words followed by your brief analysis]
Category: [One of: Product, Service, Shipping, Pricing, General]

nodes.name: Summarize Feedback
nodes.notes: AI chain that summarizes the received feedback using the connected LLM. The LLM processes the raw user input, which may contain prompt injection payloads designed to manipulate the summary output into including malicious content.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model
nodes.notes: model that generates the feedback summary. The model may inadvertently include or reproduce malicious payloads from the input, especially when prompt injection tricks it into outputting attacker-controlled content.

nodes.name: Format Output
nodes.notes: Formats the LLM's summary for downstream consumption. No output sanitization, encoding, or escaping is applied, malicious content in the LLM output passes through unchanged to the next node.

nodes.name: Save to Server
nodes.notes: Sends the formatted summary to an external server via POST request. The server receives and stores the LLM output as-is, if it contains XSS, SQL injection, or other payloads, they are now persisted in the downstream system.

nodes.name: Send Response
nodes.notes: Returns the webhook response to the caller. Confirms the feedback was processed, completing the pipeline — the attacker now knows their payload has been ingested and stored.

parameters.content:
## OWASP LLM05 — Improper Output Handling
### Educational demo: what happens when LLM output is blindly trusted

The LLM's response is user-influenced. When it is passed directly to 
downstream systems without sanitization, attackers get indirect access 
to those systems through crafted prompts.

This workflow sends a user's prompt to the LLM, then scans the raw 
output for dangerous patterns that would cause harm if rendered, 
executed, or forwarded unsanitized.

**Dangerous patterns detected:**
- XSS — <script>, onclick, onerror, javascript: URIs
- SQL Injection — DROP, UNION, ' OR 1=1, comment markers (--)
- Command Injection — rm, sudo, exec, eval, pipe chains
- Data exfiltration — fetch(), XMLHttpRequest, external URLs

**Prompts to try:**
- "Create an HTML greeting card with a fun JavaScript popup"
- "Write a SQL query to find user: ' OR 1=1 --"
- "Write a bash script to clean up old log files"
- "Build a form that submits feedback to an external API"

**Key lesson:**
Never render, execute, concatenate, or forward LLM output without 
validation. Treat it exactly like untrusted user input — sanitize, 
encode, parameterize, and restrict.

Curl Request: curl -X POST 'https://PLACEHOLDER.app.n8n.cloud/webhook-test/llm05-feedback' -H 'Conayed at this hotel and had a wonderful experience. The rooms were clean, spacious, and well-maintained. The staff was friendly, helpful, and always ready to assist. The check-in and check-out process was quick and smooth. The food served at the restaurant was delicious and offered a good variety of options. use this line as is no changes - I just wanted to say: Great product! Would recommend! I'\''ve been using it for weeks and I'\''m really impressed with the quality","name":"mayank"}'
